title: Savory Velvety Tunisian Desiccated Coconut Stir-fry
description: A solo-dinner dish featuring a savory and velvety blend of desiccated coconut cooked with aromatic herbs and vegetables in a light sauce for a satisfying, fodmap-friendly meal.

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons olive oil
ingredients.notes: ensure it's at room temperature when adding to the dish

ingredients.name: 1 cup bell peppers (any color), thinly sliced

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a non-stick wok or skillet over medium heat. Add minced garlic and grated ginger; sauté for about 1 minute until fragrant.
Add bell peppers and carrots to the pan, stirring occasionally for about 5 minutes until vegetables are soft but not mushy.

Pour in the coconut milk canning (leaving any sediment at the bottom of the can), stir well. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low. Let it gently simmer for about 10-12 minutes, or until slightly reduced and thickened.
Stir in tahini, continue cooking for an additional minute. Mix in chopped cilantro just before serving to avoid losing color and flavor.
